mission.title: Install and Configure Spiderpool on Kubernetes
mission.description: Spiderpool is an underlay and RDMA network solution for Kubernetes, designed to enhance networking capabilities for bare metal, virtual machines, and public cloud environments. Installing Spiderpool allows you to leverage its high-performance networking features, particularly beneficial for network I/O-intensive applications.

steps.title: Add the Spiderpool Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Spiderpool Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add spiderpool https://spidernet-io.github.io/spiderpool/charts
```

steps.title: Update Helm repositories
steps.description:
Update your local Helm chart repository cache to ensure you have the latest version of the Spiderpool chart:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Spiderpool using Helm
steps.description:
Install Spiderpool in the `kube-system` namespace with the specified version:
```bash
helm install spiderpool spiderpool/spiderpool --version 1.0.5 --namespace kube-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Spiderpool pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep spiderpool
```

steps.title: Configure Spiderpool settings
steps.description:
You can customize the Spiderpool configuration by editing the ConfigMap. For example, to enable IPv6, use:
```bash
kubectl edit configmap spiderpool-conf -n kube-system
```
Then set `enableIPv6: true` in the configuration.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Spiderpool will show the Spiderpool pods running in the `kube-system` namespace. You can further customize the configuration as needed to suit your network requirements.
codeSnippets: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep spiderpool

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed.
